solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxConsecutiveAnswers(String answerKey, int k) {
        int maxConsecutive = 0;

        // Function to find max window with at most k changes for a specific character
        java.util.function.Function<Character, Integer> slidingWindow = (target) -> {
            int left = 0, changes = 0, maxLen = 0;
            for (int right = 0; right < answerKey.length(); right++) {
                if (answerKey.charAt(right) != target) {
                    changes++;
                }
                while (changes > k) {
                    if (answerKey.charAt(left++) != target) {
                        changes--;
                    }
                }
                maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, right - left + 1);
            }
            return maxLen;
        };

        int maxT = slidingWindow.apply('T');
        int maxF = slidingWindow.apply('F');

        return Math.max(maxT, maxF);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a sliding window technique to find the maximum length of consecutive characters by allowing up to k changes. It separately computes the maximum for 'T' and 'F' and returns the larger of the two. The sliding window ensures linear time complexity and constant space usage.
